capabilities: Ensure event store design is robust by integrating derived knowledge bases, focusing on error minimization and operation efficiency.

procedure.action: Compose the meta.protocol derived knowledge base with the event store by mapping protocol fields to event store schema; ensure all fields are covered by schema validation rules by composing module interfaces via adapter pattern; assert input/output type contracts match before connecting; assert integration_error_count < 1 after contract validation.
Execute a batch of 1000 event insertions into the event store and track error_count; assert error_count < 1 to ensure insertion reliability.
Measure operation_duration_ms for each event insertion and log to audit trail; ensure p95 of operation_duration_ms < 50ms to verify performance efficiency.

Specify success criteria using domain-specific metrics such as event throughput and latency; ensure these metrics align with system SLAs by specifying schema, constraints, and invariants in machine-readable form; include at least one falsifiable acceptance criterion where error_rate < 0.001.

Assert the result of event processing by checking result_valid = true for all processed events; log result_code to audit trail for traceability by asserting all required fields conform to the schema definition; emit VALIDATION_FAILED with field path on the first failing field; assert field_validation_pass_rate >= 1.0.

summary: Evaluate event store design by integrating meta.protocol derived knowledge base, focusing on criteria, metrics, and validation steps for success measurement.
title: Event Store Design Evaluation

failureConditions: High error rates when derived knowledge base fields are not correctly mapped, causing schema validation failures.
Performance degradation when operation_duration_ms exceeds acceptable thresholds, leading to SLA violations.
Inconsistent event processing results when result_valid checks are bypassed, causing data integrity issues.
metrics: input/output type contracts match before connecting
the result of event processing by checking result_valid = true for all processed events
successConditions: result_valid = true for all processed events
